features.summary: Eli Lilly reported strong Q2 2021 results with 23% revenue growth (20% constant currency), driven entirely by 22 percentage points of volume growth. Core business excluding COVID-19 antibodies grew 12% for the quarter and 11% for the first half. Key growth products including Trulicity, Taltz, Verzenio, and Jardiance represented 54% of core business revenue and drove strong performance across major geographies. Operating margin expanded 140 basis points to 29.4%, with continued margin expansion expected in the second half.
features.management_outlook: Management expressed confidence in full-year 2021 guidance and long-term prospects, raising core business revenue outlook by $200 million while lowering COVID-19 antibody revenue expectations. The company expects continued recovery from pandemic impacts with healthcare systems returning to normal and new-to-brand prescriptions tracking above pre-COVID baselines in most therapeutic areas. Management plans to provide 2022 guidance at a December investor meeting and remains focused on pipeline execution with multiple late-stage opportunities advancing.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 pandemic variability affecting global markets, pricing pressures across the portfolio with expected low single-digit U.S. price declines, and regulatory uncertainties around key pipeline assets. Specific concerns include FDA delays for Olumiant in atopic dermatitis due to JAK inhibitor class review, potential Medicare coverage limitations for Alzheimer's treatments, and the need for confirmatory data for donanemab and other pipeline candidates to maintain long-term commercial success.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include strong volume performance of key brands (Trulicity, Taltz, Verzenio, Jardiance) gaining market share in expanding classes, successful pipeline advancement with planned regulatory submissions for tirzepatide in diabetes and donanemab in Alzheimer's by year-end, and positive late-stage results including Jardiance in heart failure. Geographic expansion, particularly in China with 35% growth excluding Cialis divestiture, and margin expansion from revenue growth outpacing expense increases are additional key value drivers.
